title: Bleekeria minima
intro: Bleekeria minima, also known as Kirtisingha, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical forests. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bleekeria minima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bleekeria minima is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Bleekeria minima is a species of marine fish, specifically a type of goby. It belongs to the family Oxudercinae. Oxudercinae is a subfamily within the goby family Gobiidae. The Gobiidae family is part of the larger order Perciformes, which includes many different species of fish. This classification provides a sense of the Bleekeria minima's evolutionary relationships within the larger context of fish diversity.
